m1-intro.question: Which rocks form from cooling magma?
m1-intro.explanation: Correct! Igneous rocks crystallize as magma cools.
hints: Think about what 'igneous' literally means in Latin.
One family forms from cooled liquid rock, one from squashed layers, one from squeezed-and-heated older rock.

m2-igneous.question: Coarse-grained igneous rocks (like granite) form when magma cools:
m2-igneous.explanation: Correct! Slow cooling deep down gives crystals time to grow large.
hints: Big crystals need time. Where on Earth do they get the most time?

m2-sed.question: Which process turns loose sediment into solid sedimentary rock?
hints: It's a two-step: squeeze, then glue.

m3-ptt.question: A shale turned into slate has undergone:
m3-ptt.explanation: Correct! Heat + pressure without melting = metamorphism.
hints: It didn't melt, and it didn't break down — it changed form.

m1-wave.question: P-waves and S-waves differ mainly in:
m1-wave.explanation: Correct! P-waves are longitudinal and faster; S-waves are transverse and slower.

m2-grav.question: A gravity high at the surface most likely indicates:
m2-grav.explanation: Correct! Denser subsurface rock = stronger pull = positive anomaly.
hints: More mass below = stronger pull above.

m2-mag.question: Magnetic surveys are especially useful for mapping:
m2-mag.explanation: Correct! Magnetite-bearing basement rocks give the strongest magnetic signal.
hints: Which rocks hold onto magnetic minerals best?

m3-design.question: The first step in any geophysical survey design is:
m3-design.explanation: Correct! Method follows the question, not the other way around.
hints: The method is a tool, not the goal.

m1-smelt.question: A flux in smelting is added mainly to:
options: Form a slag that traps impurities
m1-smelt.explanation: Correct! Flux + impurities = slag, which is skimmed off.
hints: You want a separate liquid layer to carry the waste away.

m2-leach.question: Leaching kinetics are usually fastest when the ore is:
m2-leach.explanation: Correct! More surface area = faster reaction.
hints: Surface area is the key lever in heterogeneous reactions.

m2-sx.question: Solvent extraction separates metals primarily based on:
options: Differential solubility in two immiscible liquids
m2-sx.explanation: Correct! Each metal prefers a different phase.
hints: It's literally 'this metal likes oil, that one likes water.'

m3-ew.question: In electrowinning, the target metal deposits on the:
m3-ew.explanation: Correct! Positive ions gain electrons at the cathode.

options: Solid, inorganic, defined chemistry, crystal structure
questions.explanation: A mineral must be a naturally occurring inorganic solid with a defined chemical composition and crystal structure.

questions.prompt: Which is the most abundant mineral group in Earth's crust?
questions.explanation: Silicates dominate the crust (quartz, feldspar, mica, olivine, etc.).

questions.prompt: The hardest mineral on the Mohs scale is:
questions.explanation: Diamond = 10, the top of the scale.

questions.prompt: Match the mineral to its system.

questions.explanation: Calcite is a carbonate.

options: How a mineral breaks along planes of weakness
questions.explanation: Cleavage is the tendency to split along smooth planar surfaces.

questions.prompt: Streak is the color of a mineral's:
questions.explanation: Streak is observed by scraping the mineral across an unglazed plate.

options: How light reflects off a mineral
questions.explanation: Luster is the optical reflectance (metallic, vitreous, pearly, etc.).
Metamorphism is solid-state change driven by heat and pressure, without melting.

options: Aligned mineral grains from directed pressure
questions.explanation: Foliation comes from preferred mineral alignment under directed stress.
Contact metamorphism happens in the aureole around an intrusion.

questions.prompt: Slate is the metamorphic equivalent of:
questions.explanation: Slate forms from low-grade metamorphism of shale.
Grade reflects the intensity of T and P the rock experienced.
P-waves compress and stretch along travel direction.
S-waves need shear strength, which fluids lack.
Speed change at an interface bends the wave.
Refraction maps velocity contrasts in the shallow subsurface.
S-waves can't pass through the liquid outer core.
Surface waves cause the bulk of earthquake damage.

questions.prompt: A gravity anomaly is a difference between:
questions.explanation: Anomalies are residuals after subtracting a model.

questions.prompt: A positive magnetic anomaly usually means:
questions.explanation: Magnetite strongly raises the local field.

questions.prompt: Survey line spacing is mainly chosen based on:
questions.explanation: Resolution requirements dictate line spacing.

questions.prompt: GPS is used in geophysical surveys to:
questions.explanation: Position is metadata essential to all geophysical data.

options: Elevation and mass between station and datum
questions.explanation: It removes the gravitational effect of rock between you and datum.
Total field or component magnetometers are the workhorses.

questions.prompt: Match the method to what it maps best.

options: Repeating stations and checking for drift
questions.explanation: Repeat readings, base stations, and drift checks are standard QC.
Pyro = fire, i.e. high-temperature processing.
Flux + gangue = slag that floats and is tapped off.

options: A molten sulfide phase rich in copper
questions.explanation: Matte is an intermediate Cu-Fe sulfide melt.

options: Air or oxygen to oxidize iron sulfide
questions.explanation: Pearce-Smith and PS converters blow air/oxygen to remove Fe and S.
Roasting oxidizes sulfides to oxides or sulfates.
Slag chemistry and temperature control viscosity.

questions.prompt: Refractory lining in a furnace is chosen for:
options: Chemical and thermal compatibility with the process
questions.explanation: Linings must resist corrosion, erosion, and heat.
Metal ions plate onto the cathode from purified solution.

options: An impure anode and a pure cathode
questions.explanation: Impure metal dissolves and pure metal plates.

options: Noble metals like Au, Ag, Pt
questions.explanation: Noble metals don't dissolve and collect as slime.
Real deposition vs Faraday's law prediction.
All three contribute to the cell voltage.

questions.prompt: A short circuit in a cell:
options: Wastes energy and can damage hardware
questions.explanation: Shorts bypass useful electrochemical work.

questions.prompt: Match each rock family to its origin.
questions.explanation: Igneous forms from cooling magma. (Sedimentary and metamorphic pairings are swapped.)

questions.prompt: A coarse-grained granite most likely cooled:
questions.explanation: Slow cooling → large crystals.

questions.prompt: Match each mineral to its diagnostic property.
Which is NOT a silicate mineral?
questions.explanation: Calcite is a carbonate.

questions.prompt: Cleavage in mica is described as:
questions.explanation: Mica splits into thin flexible sheets.

questions.prompt: The mineral with the highest Mohs hardness is:
questions.explanation: Diamond = 10 on the Mohs scale.
S-waves need shear strength, which fluids lack.

questions.prompt: Match the method to the property it measures.
A positive magnetic anomaly usually means:
questions.explanation: Magnetite strongly raises the local field.
Method follows the question.

options: The gravitational effect of rock above datum
questions.explanation: It corrects for the rock between station and datum.

questions.prompt: In electrowinning, the target metal plates on the:
questions.explanation: Cations are reduced at the cathode.
Noble metals don't dissolve.

questions.prompt: A flux in smelting is added to:
questions.explanation: Flux + gangue = slag.
Real metal vs Faraday prediction.

rock-cycle.title: Build a mineral exploration strategy
rock-cycle.description: Analyze real geological datasets, build structural models, propose an exploration plan.
rubric: Economic feasibility of the proposed plan
rock-cycle.reviewNote: Reviewed by mentors + peers. You'll review 2 others' projects too (earns XP & reputation).

geophysics-intro.title: Design a multi-method survey
geophysics-intro.description: Given a target and a budget, choose and justify a combination of geophysical methods, lay out the survey, and interpret a synthetic dataset.
geophysics-intro.reviewNote: Reviewed by mentors + peers. You'll review 2 others' projects too.

materials.title: Design a flowsheet for a low-grade ore
materials.description: Take a low-grade polymetallic ore and propose a complete extraction flowsheet — from comminution to refined metal.
materials.reviewNote: Reviewed by mentors + peers. You'll review 2 others' projects too.

reviewCards.front: What process turns sediment into rock?
reviewCards.back: Lithification — compaction and cementation of sediments under pressure.

reviewCards.front: Which mineral group dominates the crust?
Foliation in metamorphic rocks comes from…
reviewCards.back: Preferred alignment of mineral grains under directed pressure.

reviewCards.front: Why can't S-waves travel through the outer core?
reviewCards.back: S-waves need shear strength; liquids don't support shear.

reviewCards.front: A positive gravity anomaly most likely indicates…
reviewCards.back: Denser rock closer to the surface than the model assumed.

reviewCards.front: Why is a flux added in smelting?
reviewCards.back: To combine with gangue minerals and form a separable slag.

reviewCards.front: What collects in the anode slime of an electrorefining cell?
reviewCards.back: Noble metals (Au, Ag, Pt) that don't dissolve anodically.
